keywords.serpWeakness: Top results often gate the calculator behind a lead form, ask for personal information, or are simple single-screen tools without amortization export.
userComplaints: Asks for email address before showing results.
Does not display a full amortization schedule with extra payments.

keywords.serpWeakness: Many calculators only support regular extra payments, not one‑time lump sums; the UI is cluttered and not mobile‑friendly.
userComplaints: No option to add one‑time extra payments (e.g., tax refund).
Cannot compare multiple overpayment scenarios side‑by‑side.

keywords.keyword: biweekly mortgage calculator with extra payments
keywords.serpWeakness: Few tools combine bi‑weekly scheduling with discretionary extra payments; most bi‑weekly calculators only show the accelerated payoff without manual overrides.
userComplaints: Does not let me apply an extra amount on top of bi‑weekly payments.
Can’t see how much faster I’d pay off compared to a standard monthly plan.

keywords.keyword: mortgage payoff calculator with extra payments amortization schedule
keywords.serpWeakness: Many calculators offer a schedule but do not allow CSV/Excel export; others hide the schedule behind a pop‑up or email wall.
userComplaints: Cannot download the amortization table as a spreadsheet.
The schedule does not separate interest saved from principal reduction clearly.

keywords.keyword: how much can I save by paying extra on my mortgage
keywords.serpWeakness: Top articles are text‑heavy with static examples and no embedded interactive calculator; users bounce because they can’t enter their own numbers.
userComplaints: Articles only show generic examples, not my actual loan details.
No quick interactive widget to see savings instantly.

keywords.serpWeakness: Many US‑specific calculators ignore common American features like PMI removal or escrow adjustments, giving unrealistic payment forecasts.
userComplaints: Does not factor in when PMI will drop off, skewing future payment amounts.
Real monthly payment includes taxes and insurance, but calculator only shows principal+interest.

keywords.serpWeakness: Existing tools are mostly basic widgets from small blogs; they rarely allow irregular extra payments or variable interest rates.
userComplaints: Can only add a fixed extra amount every month, not random lump sums.
No way to model an adjustable‑rate mortgage with extra payments.

keywords.keyword: free mortgage overpayment calculator no email
keywords.serpWeakness: Explicit searches for ‘no email’ have very few dedicated landing pages; most sites still force email collection.
userComplaints: Every calculator I try forces me to enter an email address to see the full results.
After entering email, I get spammed with lender calls.

keywords.serpWeakness: Top results are from template sites with outdated designs and no guidance; some require payment for full features.
userComplaints: Downloaded Excel template is broken on newer versions of Excel.
No instructions on how to change loan parameters properly.

keywords.serpWeakness: Listicles are often affiliate‑heavy and recommend tools that ask for personal data; reviews are not based on real user testing.
userComplaints: Review sites just list the same big‑brand calculators without testing them.
The ‘best’ calculators still require sign‑ups, which defeats the purpose.

keywords.keyword: mortgage overpayment calculator with lump sum payments
keywords.serpWeakness: Very few calculators allow entering multiple irregular lump sums at specific future dates; most only accept a single extra‑monthly amount.
userComplaints: I want to model yearly lump sums from bonuses, but the tool doesn’t allow it.
Can’t schedule a future one‑time payment and see its impact.

keywords.keyword: how does paying extra on mortgage work
keywords.serpWeakness: Articles often bury the explanation in financial jargon and lack visual examples; no interactive ‘what‑if’ tool to solidify understanding.
userComplaints: Too much finance terminology – I just want to see how an extra $200/month helps.
No visual graph showing interest saved over time.

keywords.keyword: mortgage calculator with extra payments biweekly
keywords.serpWeakness: Existing bi‑weekly calculators rarely let you combine both bi‑weekly acceleration and additional extra payments, limiting comparison options.
userComplaints: Can’t see side‑by‑side comparison of bi‑weekly only vs bi‑weekly + extra $100.
The amortization schedule does not reflect the different payment frequency.

designConfig.voice: Professional, trustworthy — like a financial advisor explaining things over coffee. No jargon, no scare tactics. Just clear numbers.
designConfig.tagline: See exactly when you will be mortgage-free.
